frame_analyses.description: Initial view of FLS trainer box with foam pegs visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup phase, no instruments visible yet

frame_analyses.description: Needle drivers visible approaching target, purple suture already loaded
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp visible on instrument

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver placing suture through first mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle entry technique, appropriate angle

frame_analyses.description: Needle emerging through second mark on drain, purple suture visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good follow-through, both marks appear targeted correctly

frame_analyses.description: Extracorporeal knot tying in progress, hands visible outside trainer
Knot formation continues with both suture ends being manipulated
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good control of both suture ends

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher being introduced to advance knot down to tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate use of knot pusher instrument

frame_analyses.description: View of Penrose drain with knot being advanced, inset shows close-up
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot positioning appears accurate to target area

frame_analyses.description: Additional throws being added to complete knot sequence
Final view showing completed suture placement before cutting

penalties.description: Entry point appears approximately 1mm lateral to first mark

confidence_rationale: Good visualization of major phases and knot placement. Some uncertainty in precise mm measurements of suture deviation due to camera angles. Final knot appears secure with no visible gaps.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation measurements — camera angle makes precise measurement challenging
Whether suture was completely cut at the end — video cuts to black during final phase
technique_summary: Strong intermediate performance with efficient needle loading and placement phases. Primary time expenditure in knot formation and advancement phases, which is typical for this task.
strengths: Clean needle loading and placement technique
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise targeting of marks to reduce deviation penalties
Practice knot formation speed while maintaining security
